description: Usage comment documents `curl -fsSL .../install.sh | bash` as the standard installation method with no integrity check — users who follow the docs execute arbitrary code from GitHub
suggested_fix: Replace with a checksummed installation: fetch a pinned release tarball and verify its SHA-256 before executing, rather than piping curl to bash

description: Usage comment documents `curl -fsSL .../quick-install.sh | bash` as the standard installation method with no integrity check — same supply-chain risk as install.sh
suggested_fix: Add SHA-256 verification step or document a checksummed install path alongside the curl | bash shortcut

description: Downloads remote `airis-gateway` shell script from GitHub raw URL and immediately executes it (chmod +x at line 119, executed at line 129) without any hash verification — functionally equivalent to curl | bash
suggested_fix: Fetch a SHA-256 sidecar from the release, verify digest before chmod+execute; or bundle airis-gateway into the release archive and verify the archive

description: Downloads `airis_bootstrap.py` from GitHub raw URL without integrity check; the file is executed at line 358 via `python3 "$DIR/scripts/airis_bootstrap.py"` — remote code execution if the GitHub repo or CDN is compromised
suggested_fix: Pin download URL to a tagged release, fetch SHA-256 sidecar and verify before executing the Python script

description: `curl -fsSL "$url" | tar -xz -C "$BIN_DIR"` downloads and unpacks the airis-workspace binary from GitHub releases without checksum or signature verification
suggested_fix: Download a .sha256 file from the same release, verify with `sha256sum -c` before extracting the tarball

description: `curl -fsSL "$url" | tar -xz -C "$install_dir"` downloads airis-workspace binary without checksum verification — same pattern as install.sh:163
suggested_fix: Verify SHA-256 of the tarball before extraction

description: `server = '$server'` interpolates the shell variable directly into a python3 -c heredoc; a server name containing a single quote or command substitution can corrupt or hijack the embedded Python code in cmd_enable (same pattern at line 310 in cmd_disable)
suggested_fix: Pass the value as a positional argument: `python3 -c '...' "$server"` and read from `sys.argv[1]` inside the script instead of interpolating into the code string

description: `VERSION="${AIRIS_VERSION:-latest}"` defaults to `latest` — any future push to the main/latest tag silently changes what new installs receive, with no pinned version in the distributed script
suggested_fix: Default to a pinned release tag (e.g., `v0.2.0`) and update it on each release; keep env-var override for advanced users

description: `BASE_URL` is constructed with `${AIRIS_VERSION:-latest}` — all remote file downloads (docker-compose, configs, scripts) resolve against the latest tag without a pinned version
suggested_fix: Embed a default pinned version matching the CLI release; update on each release

description: Four workflow rules listed as unordered bullets instead of numbered steps — multi-step commands should use numbered steps for unambiguous ordering
suggested_fix: Convert the Rules: bullet list to a numbered list (1. 2. 3. 4.)

description: No output format section — command does not specify what form the final response should take (report, checklist, code, etc.)
suggested_fix: Add an ## Output section specifying what the LLM should produce after completing the pre-flight inspection

description: Vague directive: "prefer Playwright CLI guidance" — "prefer" does not specify when the preference applies or how strong it is
suggested_fix: Replace with a concrete condition: "If the task involves browser automation, use Playwright CLI instead of MCP"

description: No example blocks — skill provides no concrete before/after examples of the workflow in action
suggested_fix: Add at least one ## Example section showing a real query scenario with the step-by-step application

description: Vague quantifier: "appropriate caution" — does not specify what caution level is appropriate for a given operation
suggested_fix: Replace with the explicit caution table already present in the file; remove the vague phrase from the surrounding prose

description: No example blocks — skill has no concrete debugging scenario walkthrough
suggested_fix: Add one example showing the gather → narrow → investigate → verify cycle applied to a real debugging scenario

description: No example blocks — skill has no concrete implementation walkthrough
suggested_fix: Add one example showing the docs-first → patterns → implement → verify cycle for a specific library integration

description: No example blocks — skill has no concrete research scenario walkthrough
suggested_fix: Add one example showing the identify → official-docs → web-search → synthesize cycle for a specific research question

description: Command uses $ARGUMENTS for issue type but has no empty-input branch — when invoked with no argument, "Issue Type: " renders blank with no guidance on how to proceed
suggested_fix: Add a leading check: if no $ARGUMENTS, list the common issue types and prompt the user to re-invoke with one

description: Vague quantifier: "at least some servers are 'ready'" — does not specify the minimum count or which servers are required
suggested_fix: Specify the expected servers by name or a minimum count: e.g., "Confirm airis-mcp-gateway-control and airis-commands are ready"

description: Vague qualifier: "actionable troubleshooting steps" — does not specify what makes a step actionable
suggested_fix: Remove the qualifier or replace with a concrete format: "include exact commands the user can run to resolve each failure"

description: `Bash(curl*)` is declared in allowed-tools but the command body contains only `task *` invocations — no direct curl call is shown
suggested_fix: Remove `Bash(curl*)` from allowed-tools if health checks run through task commands, or add an explicit curl health-check step to the command body

description: Vague qualifier: "Explain the chosen route briefly" — "briefly" is subjective and does not constrain output length or format
suggested_fix: Replace with a concrete instruction: "In one sentence, state the chosen route and the primary reason"

description: Vague quantifier: "Saves significant tokens per `initialize`" — "significant" is unquantified
suggested_fix: Replace with a concrete figure if known, e.g., "reduces tools/list payload by ~40%", or remove the qualifier

description: Skill directs users to invoke `superpowers:systematic-debugging` before using this skill, but `superpowers` is not declared as a dependency in `skills/.claude-plugin/plugin.json` — users without superpowers installed receive broken workflow guidance
suggested_fix: Add a fallback paragraph for when superpowers is not installed, or declare the dependency in plugin.json
